MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gina Raeta, Esq. (415-986-7300) for Plaintiff/opposing party .
Kenneth Downs, Esq. (415-453-9999) for Defendant/ Moving party James Nguyen.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court does not believe PLaintiff properly served. Defendant does not contest. Plaintiff must read the local rules on substitute service. Plaintiff requests to have a special server appointed. The Court Denies the request without prejudice. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JAMES NGUYEN's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is GRANTED. Non-registered process server failed to sufficiently exercise due diligence in his attempts to effectuate proper service in accordance with California laws and San Francisco Local Rules of Court. Order is signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
